title: The rise of industrial capitalism
description: Practice your skills in this exercise on the rise of industrial capitalism.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below.

> “Of the numerous other tariffs increased to protectionist levels after the war, the most abusive examples were the treatment of steel rails and nickel. In the case of steel rails, Congress changed the tariff in 1870 . . . to a specific duty based on weight, which came to \$28 per ton. The effect of this change was astronomical due to new efficiencies then developing in the steel industry . . . which caused the price of steel to drop dramatically after 1870. . . . At a time of high demand for railroad materials in the late 1870s, during a period of explosive growth of rail lines, these domestic producers made out like bandits—or, more appropriately, robber barons—until the demand finally tapered off.” 

>-Source: C. Donald Johnson, historian, *The Wealth of a Nation: A History of Trade Politics in America,* 2018

**The excerpt best illustrates which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of government policies that supported US economic growth
the rise in technological innovations to improve manufacturing techniques
the supremacy of the Democratic party in holding the office of the presidency
the influence of labor unions and political machines on foreign trade policies

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below.

> “Of the numerous other tariffs increased to protectionist levels after the war, the most abusive examples were the treatment of steel rails and nickel. In the case of steel rails, Congress changed the tariff in 1870 . . . to a specific duty based on weight, which came to \$28 per ton. The effect of this change was astronomical due to new efficiencies then developing in the steel industry . . . which caused the price of steel to drop dramatically after 1870. . . . At a time of high demand for railroad materials in the late 1870s, during a period of explosive growth of rail lines, these domestic producers made out like bandits—or, more appropriately, robber barons—until the demand finally tapered off.” 

>-Source: C. Donald Johnson, historian, *The Wealth of a Nation: A History of Trade Politics in America,* 2018

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that tariffs had on economic growth during the Gilded Age?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It increased the price of foreign goods and supported the sale of American-made goods.
It increased foreign trade with the United States, opening up new markets in Asia and Latin America.
It led to the development of the first transcontinental railroad as the price of steel dropped.
It led to a decline in food prices, plunging the United States into a financial panic.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below.

> “Of the numerous other tariffs increased to protectionist levels after the war, the most abusive examples were the treatment of steel rails and nickel. In the case of steel rails, Congress changed the tariff in 1870 . . . to a specific duty based on weight, which came to \$28 per ton. The effect of this change was astronomical due to new efficiencies then developing in the steel industry . . . which caused the price of steel to drop dramatically after 1870. . . . At a time of high demand for railroad materials in the late 1870s, during a period of explosive growth of rail lines, these domestic producers made out like bandits—or, more appropriately, robber barons—until the demand finally tapered off.” 

>-Source: C. Donald Johnson, historian, *The Wealth of a Nation: A History of Trade Politics in America,* 2018

**The developments described in the excerpt most directly reflect which of the following changes in the late nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rapid economic development brought on by pro-growth government policies
the rise of strikes and protests to oppose the concentration of wealth
the expansion of US businesses into foreign markets to sell manufactured goods
the improvements in manufacturing processes for steel and other metals

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below.

> “The rise of modern mass production required fundamental changes in the technology and organization of the processes of production. The basic organizational innovations were responses to the need to coordinate and control the high-volume throughput.”

>-Source: Alfred D. Chandler, historian, *The Visible Hand: The Managerial Revolution in American Business,* 1977

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that mass production had on business practices?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It shifted management techniques to handle the increase in the volume of goods produced in factories.
It decreased the price of goods and caused business owners to expand into foreign markets.
It caused technological innovation to improve manufacturing techniques and increase production.
It triggered the consolidation of businesses as smaller companies could not produce at the same rate.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below.

> “The rise of modern mass production required fundamental changes in the technology and organization of the processes of production. The basic organizational innovations were responses to the need to coordinate and control the high-volume throughput.”

>-Source: Alfred D. Chandler, historian, *The Visible Hand: The Managerial Revolution in American Business,* 1977

**Which of the following most likely contributed to the rise of “modern mass production” referred to in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of new efficient technology
the entrance into new foreign markets
the acquisition of foreign countries
the improvements in advertising

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below.

> “The rise of modern mass production required fundamental changes in the technology and organization of the processes of production. The basic organizational innovations were responses to the need to coordinate and control the high-volume throughput.”

>-Source: Alfred D. Chandler, historian, *The Visible Hand: The Managerial Revolution in American Business,* 1977

**One major change in American factories from 1850 to 1900 was the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: use of bureaucratic management structures in factories.
end of the use of children as the factory labor force.
prohibition of trusts and monopolies by the government.
introduction of new technologies to replace most workers.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“In whatever direction we look in any section of our vast republic, we find prodigious combinations of railway capital actually perfected, or progressing rapidly toward complete absorption of all smaller lines. This tendency to consolidation is irresistible. The economic advantages are so great, the temptations to vast profits are so incalculable, and the allurements to ambitious and able men afforded by the control of these mighty social machines are so enticing, that the movement must go forward.”

>-Source: George Henry Lewis, *National Consolidation of the Railways of the United States,* 1893

**Which of the following issues of the period was the author most likely concerned with in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the practice of business owners who acquired smaller businesses to create bigger ones
the increasing costs for farmers and small business owners to transport their goods on railroads
the acquisition of territory by the United States, expanding to the Pacific Ocean
the policy decision to increase tariffs on foreign goods to improve the United States’ economy

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“In whatever direction we look in any section of our vast republic, we find prodigious combinations of railway capital actually perfected, or progressing rapidly toward complete absorption of all smaller lines. This tendency to consolidation is irresistible. The economic advantages are so great, the temptations to vast profits are so incalculable, and the allurements to ambitious and able men afforded by the control of these mighty social machines are so enticing, that the movement must go forward.”

>-Source: George Henry Lewis, *National Consolidation of the Railways of the United States,* 1893

**The events discussed in the excerpt led to which of the following economic changes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the concentration of wealth to a small group of business owners
the end of tariffs to support struggling farmers during a financial panic
the regulation of railroad prices by the federal government
the creation of government subsidies to support smaller railroad companies

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“In whatever direction we look in any section of our vast republic, we find prodigious combinations of railway capital actually perfected, or progressing rapidly toward complete absorption of all smaller lines. This tendency to consolidation is irresistible. The economic advantages are so great, the temptations to vast profits are so incalculable, and the allurements to ambitious and able men afforded by the control of these mighty social machines are so enticing, that the movement must go forward.”

>-Source: George Henry Lewis, *National Consolidation of the Railways of the United States,* 1893

**The excerpt most directly expresses an economic perspective that:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: opposed government regulation of the economy.
supported legislation to ban monopolies.
opposed the use of children as a labor force.
supported setting national standards of safety.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“For the means of finding new productive employments for capital, therefore, it is necessary that the great industrial countries should turn to countries which have not felt the pulse of modern progress. Such countries have yet to be equipped with the mechanism of production and of luxury, which has been created in the progressive countries by the savings of recent generations. . . . Existing commodities now imported from the interior of these countries at great cost will be swept on paths of steel to the seacoast with the result of reducing their cost, increasing their consumption, and benefitting at once both producer and purchaser.”

>-Source: Charles A. Conant, *North American Review,* 1898

**The federal government implemented the ideas expressed in the excerpt by:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: expanding its territorial holdings beyond the continental United States.
instituting tariffs to encourage Americans to support domestic industries by buying their goods.
issuing a proclamation that the United States would protect all Latin American countries from colonization.
closing off all trade with foreign countries and returning to a period of isolationism.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“For the means of finding new productive employments for capital, therefore, it is necessary that the great industrial countries should turn to countries which have not felt the pulse of modern progress. Such countries have yet to be equipped with the mechanism of production and of luxury, which has been created in the progressive countries by the savings of recent generations. . . . Existing commodities now imported from the interior of these countries at great cost will be swept on paths of steel to the seacoast with the result of reducing their cost, increasing their consumption, and benefitting at once both producer and purchaser.”

>-Source: Charles A. Conant, *North American Review,* 1898

**The ideas discussed in the excerpt led to which of the following political changes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of favorable trade agreements with Asian and Latin American countries
the passage of a law that limited immigration from China to the United States
the establishment of an international organization that aimed to protect countries from colonization
the negotiation between the United States and Panama to create a canal to ease navigation

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“For the means of finding new productive employments for capital, therefore, it is necessary that the great industrial countries should turn to countries which have not felt the pulse of modern progress. Such countries have yet to be equipped with the mechanism of production and of luxury, which has been created in the progressive countries by the savings of recent generations. . . . Existing commodities now imported from the interior of these countries at great cost will be swept on paths of steel to the seacoast with the result of reducing their cost, increasing their consumption, and benefitting at once both producer and purchaser.”

>-Source: Charles A. Conant, *North American Review,* 1898

**Which of the following developments best represents a logical extension of the ideas expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the US acquisition of the Philippines, Puerto Rico, and Hawaii to access their natural resources and to facilitate trade
the negotiation of free trade agreements with China, Japan, and England to improve the economy
the creation of lax immigration policies to encourage emigration from China, Russia, and Italy
the commitment to an isolationist foreign policy to keep the United States out of international wars
